item.title: Bank of Japan Governor Ueda Hospitalised, Will Miss Key Policy Meeting
item.description: BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda has been hospitalised for medical treatment and will miss the June 15-16 policy meeting, the central bank announced, raising uncertainty about Japans monetary policy direction during a critical period.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Israel Kills 16 in Lebanon as UN Announces International Law Probe
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 16 people as UN investigators announced they will arrive next week to assess potential violations of international law by all parties in the escalating conflict.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: Trump Says He Loves Inflation as Prices Hit 3-Year High
item.description: President Donald Trump dismissed concerns about surging consumer prices, saying I love the inflation and claiming the U.S. is taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil, as inflation reached its highest level in three years.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: Violent Unrest Erupts in Belfast Over Knife Attack Arrest
item.description: Police in Northern Ireland used water cannons against protesters as violent unrest flared in Belfast following the arrest of a Sudanese man charged with attempted murder in a stabbing attack, with officials calling for calm.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: NATO Forward Land Forces Stand Up New Multinational Battlegroup in Finland
item.description: NATO has stood up its Forward Land Forces Finland during a ceremony at the Swedish Army base, establishing the alliances newest multinational battlegroup as part of its enhanced deterrence posture in Northern Europe.
item.dc:creator: Janes

item.title: Trump Threatens New Military Action Against Iran After Helicopter Downing
item.description: President Trump warned of additional strikes on Iran, accusing Tehran of "playing us for suckers" after a US helicopter was downed near the Strait of Hormuz. The escalation comes amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: CBS News

item.title: China Re-centers North Korea Ties as Nuclear Silence Reshapes Regional Balance
item.description: As Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang, China is reasserting its influence with North Korea at a time when Kim Jong Un has remained silent on nuclear activities. The diplomatic move signals Beijing effort to counter US pressure in the region.
item.dc:creator: NPR

item.title: US Navy Establishes Naval Support Activity in Western Australia Under AUKUS Pact
item.description: As part of the AUKUS security pact, NSA Stirling will provide support for personnel assigned to Submarine Rotational Forces, strengthening Western military presence in the Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: Defense News

item.title: Mariupol Port No Longer Operational After Ukrainian Strikes, Azov Corps Says
item.description: Russian-occupied Mariupol's port has been rendered non-operational by Ukrainian strikes, a significant blow to Russian logistics in the Azov Sea region.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: ICEYE and Rheinmetall Form German Joint Venture for Space-Based ISR
item.description: Finnish SAR satellite company ICEYE and German defense giant Rheinmetall have launched a joint venture for space-based intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Breaking Defense

item.title: US Inflation Tops 4% for First Time in Three Years as Gas Prices Surge
item.description: Consumer prices rose 4.2% in May from a year earlier, pushed higher by energy costs from the Iran war. Gas prices have fallen since mid-May but remain above $4 per gallon, squeezing American households.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: NASA Unveils Artemis III Crew to Test Technology for Future Moon Landing
item.description: NASA announced four astronauts for the Artemis III mission: Randy Bresnik, Frank Rubio, Andre Douglas and ESA astronaut Luca Parmitano. They will orbit Earth while practicing docking with lunar landers targeted for 2027.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: Bank of Japan Governor Hospitalized, Will Miss Key Policy Meeting
item.description: BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda has been hospitalized for medical treatment and will miss the June 15-16 policy meeting, where the central bank was expected to raise interest rates to 1.0% amid inflation concerns.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Iran War Fears Double Container Shipping Rates from Asia to US
item.description: The cost of shipping a container from Asia to the United States has doubled since the start of the Iran war, driven by spiking fuel prices and disruptions to global trade routes through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Two Merchant Vessels Damaged in Ukrainian Black Sea Corridor Attack
item.description: A Panamanian-flagged and a Barbadian-flagged vessel were damaged while transiting Ukraines Black Sea navigation corridor, highlighting ongoing risks to commercial shipping despite the corridors establishment.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: India Accelerates Military Revamp to Counter China and Pakistan
item.description: India is moving forward with Integrated Theatre Commands that would unite the army, navy and air force in its biggest military shake-up in decades, reflecting growing concerns about simultaneous two-front threats.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: IAEA Board Passes Resolution Demanding Iran Report Uranium Stocks
item.description: The UN nuclear watchdogs 35-nation Board of Governors passed a US-backed resolution telling Iran to declare its remaining uranium stocks, as concerns grow about Irans nuclear program amid the ongoing conflict.
item.dc:creator: Times of Israel

item.title: US Tungsten Scrap Exports to Japan Soar on Chinese Curbs
item.description: Japan-bound US shipments of recycled tungsten have surged dramatically as China restricts exports of the critical industrial metal, highlighting supply chain vulnerabilities in strategic materials.
item.dc:creator: Nikkei Asia

item.title: Israeli Lawmakers Advance Bill to Exempt Ultra-Orthodox from Military Service
item.description: Israeli MPs voted to advance legislation that would allow most ultra-Orthodox Jewish men to avoid conscription, a controversial move amid ongoing war and domestic political tensions.
item.dc:creator: Al-Monitor

item.title: US Forces Disable Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman for Blockade Violation
item.description: US Central Command announced that its forces disabled a Palau-flagged oil tanker transiting toward Iran for alleged violation of the blockade, marking the latest enforcement action in the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Anadolu Agency

item.title: US Consumer Inflation Hits Three-Year High Amid Iran War
item.description: Consumer prices rose 4.2% annually in May, the fastest pace in three years, driven by surging energy costs from the Middle East conflict. The data gives the Federal Reserve reason to keep interest rates unchanged into 2027.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: UN Chief Warns of Return to Full-Scale War in Middle East
item.description: Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warned of the risk of returning to full war in the Middle East after Iran and the United States traded strikes. The UN called for immediate de-escalation and a return to diplomatic negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Asharq Al-Awsat

item.title: EU and South Korea Sign Digital Trade Pact
item.description: The European Union and South Korea signed a digital trade agreement to facilitate digital transactions and strengthen economic ties. The pact comes as both sides seek to diversify trade relationships amid global uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: US News

item.title: Foreign Outflows Surge from Asian Equities on Middle East Risks
item.description: Asian equity markets are experiencing significant foreign outflows in June as investors retreat from risk amid escalating Middle East hostilities and a pullback in AI-linked technology stocks. The trend highlights regional vulnerability to geopolitical shocks.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah. The Iran-backed group claimed fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the border region.
item.dc:creator: Reuters
